title: Add role-based portal login landing pages for each access category

requirements.summary: Create Portal Selection page at /portal with four distinct portal entry cards for Student, Staff, Admin, and Parent portals that navigate to role-specific login pages
acceptanceCriteria: A Portal Selection page exists with four cards: Student Portal, Staff Portal, Admin Portal, and Parent Portal.
Each card has a descriptive label and an action button.
Clicking a card navigates to that role's dedicated login page (e.g., /login/student, /login/staff, /login/admin, /login/parent).
The portal selection page is accessible from the public navigation bar and the homepage portal links.
Unauthenticated visitors can access this page without logging in.

file_operations.description: Create a new public page component that displays four portal entry cards (Student, Staff, Admin, Parent). Each card includes a role title, brief description of the portal's purpose, and a button linking to the corresponding role-specific login page (/login/student, /login/staff, /login/admin, /login/parent). Use responsive grid layout with shadcn/ui Card components and maintain the existing navy-and-gold design system.
Add a new public route for /portal that renders the PortalSelectionPage component. Place this route alongside other public routes (before the authenticated role-based routes).

requirements.summary: Create four dedicated role-specific login pages with Internet Identity authentication that pre-select the corresponding role in ProfileSetupModal for new users
acceptanceCriteria: Routes /login/student, /login/staff, /login/admin, and /login/parent each render a distinct role-branded login page.
Each login page displays the role name prominently and a brief portal description.
Clicking 'Login with Internet Identity' on any of these pages triggers the standard Internet Identity authentication flow.
After successful authentication, if a profile already exists with the correct role, the user is immediately redirected to their role's dashboard (/student, /staff, /admin, or /parent).
If no profile exists yet, the ProfileSetupModal opens with the role field pre-filled and locked to the role corresponding to the login page visited.
After profile setup is completed from a role-specific login page, the user is redirected to the correct role dashboard.

If an authenticated user with an existing mismatched role lands on a role-specific login page, show a message indicating they are already registered under a different role and provide a link to their actual dashboard.

file_operations.description: Create a parameterized role-specific login page component that accepts a role prop (student, staff, admin, or parent). Display the role name and portal description prominently. Include a 'Login with Internet Identity' button that triggers the useInternetIdentity login flow. After successful authentication, check if the user has an existing profile using useGetCallerUserProfile. If a profile exists and matches the target role, redirect to the appropriate dashboard (/student, /staff, /admin, or /parent). If a profile exists but the role does not match, display a message with a link to their actual dashboard. If no profile exists, the Layout component will automatically open the ProfileSetupModal with the defaultRole prop set to the target role. Use the authorization component's authentication patterns and handle all login states (logging-in, authenticated, error).

Add four new public routes for /login/student, /login/staff, /login/admin, and /login/parent that each render the RoleLoginPage component with the corresponding role prop. Place these routes in the public section before the authenticated role-based routes.

Add an optional defaultRole prop of type UserRole to the ProfileSetupModal component. When defaultRole is provided, pre-select that role in the role dropdown and disable the role selection field (make it read-only or disabled) so the user cannot change it. Update the form initialization to use the defaultRole if present. Ensure the existing form validation and submission logic using saveCallerUserProfile mutation remains unchanged.

Update the Layout component to accept and pass through an optional defaultRole prop to the ProfileSetupModal. This allows parent components (like RoleLoginPage) to specify which role should be pre-selected when the modal opens for new users. The modal should only open when isAuthenticated is true, userProfile is null, and profile data has finished loading (isFetched is true), preventing modal flash on page load.

requirements.summary: Update homepage and Navigation component portal links to route unauthenticated visitors to role-specific login pages instead of directly to dashboards
acceptanceCriteria: Homepage portal cards and navigation portal links route unauthenticated visitors to /login/{role} pages instead of directly to protected dashboard routes.
Authenticated users with a confirmed role clicking portal links are routed directly to their dashboards.
No broken links or 404 errors occur when navigating from portal entry points.

file_operations.description: Update the portal cards section on the HomePage to link unauthenticated visitors to the new role-specific login pages (/login/student, /login/staff, /login/admin, /login/parent) instead of directly to dashboard routes. For authenticated users with an existing profile and matching role, keep the direct dashboard links. Use the isAuthenticated state from useInternetIdentity and the userProfile from useGetCallerUserProfile to determine the appropriate link destination for each portal card.

Update the portal links in the Navigation component to route unauthenticated visitors to the role-specific login pages (/login/student, /login/staff, /login/admin, /login/parent) instead of directly to protected dashboard routes. For authenticated users with a confirmed role in their profile, maintain direct navigation to their role's dashboard. Add a new 'Portals' link in the public navigation that routes to /portal (the Portal Selection page). Use isAuthenticated and userProfile state to conditionally render the correct link destinations.

Update the portal quick links in the Footer component to route unauthenticated visitors to the role-specific login pages (/login/student, /login/staff, /login/admin, /login/parent). For authenticated users with a confirmed profile role, keep direct dashboard navigation. Use the isAuthenticated state and userProfile to determine link destinations, similar to the Navigation component.
